description: Prompt-facing tool manifest. Runtime schemas remain authoritative; this file tells each mode which tools to emphasize and how to call them.
discovery_rules: The active tool schemas are already visible in this request; choose directly from them instead of calling a catalog tool.
Use `skill_lookup` only when a discovered `SKILL.md` materially changes the workflow.
Dynamic runtime-plugin tools such as `rc_*` may appear at startup; use their submitted schemas exactly.
calling_rules: Use exact tool names and exact schema field names.
Start with required fields, then add optional fields only when needed.
Prefer narrow, reversible, read-only calls before high-impact calls.
Retrieve or inspect before editing; verify after editing.
After any tool use, return a user-facing response instead of stopping at raw output.

reverie.title: Reverie Mode Tool Surface
workflow: General-purpose execution loop: understand the goal, gather evidence, choose the best available method, implement, verify, and report.
Use local repository tools for code and filesystem facts. Use web tools for current external information. Use command tools for scripts, builds, downloads, tests, and smoke checks.
Use core Blender/modeling tools directly in Reverie for 3D model creation, GLB/GLTF export planning, asset audits, and modeling workbench tasks.

For full game-production requests, playable vertical slices, balance/playtest loops, or built-in Reverie Engine work, switch to `reverie-gamer` instead of carrying the full game runtime surface in default mode.
If a task is broad, continue through safe research, implementation, repair, and verification until the requested outcome is actually delivered or a real blocker remains.
Switch modes only when another mode provides materially better workflow, artifacts, or continuity.

reverie-gamer.title: Reverie-Gamer Tool Surface
workflow: Use a prompt-to-production flow: compile request, constrain scope, create playable runtime work, verify, and iterate.
For ambitious prompts, reduce scope to the first credible playable slice before expanding.

Keep continuity artifacts current: artifacts/game_program.json, artifacts/design_intelligence.json, artifacts/campaign_program.json, artifacts/roster_strategy.json, artifacts/reference_intelligence.json, artifacts/runtime_capability_graph.json, artifacts/runtime_delivery_plan.json, artifacts/system_specs.json, artifacts/asset_pipeline.json, and playtest/slice_score.json.
Prefer concrete runtime artifacts and playtest evidence over concept-only output.

reverie-atlas.title: Reverie-Atlas Tool Surface
workflow: Research first, document the engineering contract, then implement and refresh documents after reality changes.
Use `atlas_delivery_orchestrator` for durable Atlas state, slices, blockers, checkpoints, and closure readiness.
Switch back to `reverie` for tiny, low-ambiguity coding tasks.

spec-driven.title: Spec-Driven Tool Surface
workflow: Inspect current state, create requirements, design, and tasks, then implement only after the contract is clear.
In one-shot prompt mode, continue through the requested document chain without pausing for approval.

spec-vibe.title: Spec-Vibe Tool Surface
workflow: Move quickly while still gathering enough evidence before edits.
Keep specs lightweight and useful; verify concrete outputs.

writer.title: Writer Tool Surface
workflow: For a novel or serial request, call `serial_novel` before drafting prose; infer safe creative defaults from the brief instead of forcing a questionnaire.
Use `serial_novel` to persist project bibles, chapter control cards, committed chapters, continuity ledgers, resumable progress, and verified character totals.
Use `ask_clarification` or `userInput` only when a genuinely blocking creative decision cannot be inferred from the request.
Use writer analysis tools after drafting, then commit every accepted chapter through `serial_novel`.

ant.title: Reverie-Ant Tool Surface
workflow: Structure long-running work into planning, execution, and verification phases.
Use `task_boundary` for visible phase updates and `notify_user` only when user review or a blocker matters.

computer-controller.title: Computer Controller Tool Surface
workflow: Use an observe -> decide -> act -> re-observe loop until the desktop task is complete.
Prefer one small UI action at a time and verify the screen state after each action.

codebase-retrieval.purpose: Primary Context Engine entrypoint; proactively use it for repository files, symbols, usages, dependencies, workspace memory, Git evidence, and LSP state.
codebase-retrieval.use_when: First tool for large repositories and any answer, plan, review, diagnosis, or edit that depends on repository state when the current turn has no Context Engine result yet. Prefer `task` for broad work, then use narrower queries only for unresolved details.

parameters.query_type: One of symbol, file, search, dependencies, outline, explore, task, memory, lsp. Use `task` as the default first call for broad or uncertain work.
parameters.query: The active task in the user's own words, or an exact symbol, file path, search text, or LSP target for a narrower follow-up.
parameters.direction: Optional dependency direction for dependency queries.
parameters.lsp_action: Optional LSP action such as diagnostics, definitions, document_symbols, workspace_symbols, or status.

examples: codebase-retrieval(query_type="task", query="fix streaming think tag rendering")

git-commit-retrieval.purpose: Inspect git history, blame, prior fixes, and current uncommitted state.
git-commit-retrieval.use_when: History or conventions could reduce risk.

parameters.query_type: file_history, symbol_history, blame, commit_details, search, recent, or uncommitted.
parameters.target: File path, symbol name, commit hash, or search query.

str_replace_editor.purpose: View, replace, insert, or create text files inside the workspace.
str_replace_editor.use_when: Making precise edits to existing files.

parameters.view_range: Optional [start, end] line range for view.

create_file.purpose: Create a new workspace file or intentionally overwrite an existing file.
create_file.use_when: A clean full-file create is simpler than incremental edits.

parameters.overwrite: Set true only when intentionally replacing an existing file.

file_ops.purpose: Read files, list directories, check existence, inspect metadata, or create directories.

parameters.operation: read, list, exists, info, or mkdir.

delete_file.purpose: Delete exactly one file inside the workspace.
delete_file.use_when: A file removal is required and safe.

command_exec.purpose: Run audited commands in the workspace for tests, builds, scripts, downloads, diagnostics, and git checks.
command_exec.use_when: The task needs a shell command or verification command.

web_search.purpose: Search the web for many candidate links, titles, and snippets.
web_search.use_when: Current external facts, APIs, products, docs, releases, or references may matter.

parameters.recency: Optional d, w, m, or y recency hint.
parameters.fetch_content: Compatibility option; prefer `web_fetch` for page text.

examples: web_search(query="official API documentation dependency versions", max_results=12)

web_fetch.purpose: Fetch readable content from selected URLs.
web_fetch.use_when: You need documentation details, API schemas, changelogs, metadata, or download links from known URLs.

browser_controler.purpose: Open and control Reverie's embedded open-source Chromium runtime, store all browser data under the app root .reverie/browser directory, import user-provided cookies/storage state into embedded profiles, run reusable minimized/background DevTools sessions, inspect and interact with dynamic pages through Chromium DevTools Protocol, diagnose page resources, upload files, capture screenshots, and check web/server endpoints without touching real Edge/Chrome profiles.
browser_controler.use_when: A task needs embedded browser automation, non-disruptive background frontend runtime inspection, selector-based page interaction, DevTools Console/Network/DOM/accessibility evidence, form/upload/login flow testing, dynamic page text, imported cookie/storage-state sessions, or server/API endpoint verification beyond static fetches.

parameters.action: active_window, list_browser_windows, activate_browser, open_browser, open_page, open_debug_page, open_devtools, browser_session_start/list/close/cleanup, browser_runtime_status, browser_profile_status/backup/backups/restore/import/export, devtools_targets, devtools_snapshot, devtools_screenshot, devtools_eval, devtools_console, devtools_network, devtools_click, devtools_type, devtools_upload, devtools_wait_for, devtools_accessibility_snapshot, devtools_dom_outline, devtools_find, safety_policy, diagnose_page, check_endpoint, current_url, extract_page, copy_page_text, observe, click, scroll, paste_text, key_press, hotkey, upload_file, close_page, wait, or optional open_ai_service/ai_chat.
parameters.url: Optional URL to open or extract.
parameters.port: Remote debugging port returned by open_debug_page/browser_session_start for devtools_* actions; external unrecorded ports are refused.
parameters.browser: Compatibility hint only; Browser Controler always uses the embedded Chromium runtime.
parameters.profile: Embedded browser profile name under .reverie/browser/profiles. Defaults to default.
parameters.session_id: Optional reusable Browser Controler session id.
parameters.background: For open_debug_page, keep the browser out of foreground and use CDP actions.
parameters.minimized: Open or move the browser minimized when possible.
parameters.activate: Whether to activate the browser window; use false for background runs.
parameters.selector: CSS selector for background DevTools click/type/upload/wait/find actions.
parameters.expression: JavaScript expression for devtools_eval or devtools_console.
parameters.include_bodies: Include Network response body previews for devtools_network.
parameters.include_request_body/export_har/include_: Optional Network diagnostics for payloads, HAR JSON, and WebSocket frames.
parameters.user_data_dir: Compatibility alias for profile; external profile paths are refused.
parameters.include_cache: For browser_profile_backup, include embedded profile cache folders too.
parameters.backup_id: Embedded profile backup id for browser_profile_restore.
parameters.confirm: Required true for browser_profile_restore because it writes to an embedded .reverie/browser profile.
parameters.title_contains: Optional browser window title filter for activate_browser.
parameters.include_all_windows: Include non-browser top-level windows when listing windows.
parameters.text: Text to paste/type into the active browser page.
parameters.file_path: Workspace-relative file to upload through the browser file picker.
parameters.x/y: Optional screen coordinates for click or upload targets.
parameters.check_assets: When true, diagnose_page checks linked scripts, stylesheets, and images.

examples: browser_controler(action="devtools_network", port=<session port>, url="http://localhost:3000", include_bodies=true, include_request_body=true, export_har=true)

skill_lookup.use_when: A skill may materially change the workflow.

list_mcp_resources.purpose: Discover read-only MCP resources exposed by connected servers.
list_mcp_resources.use_when: External MCP context is available as resources.

read_mcp_resource.purpose: Read a specific MCP resource by server and URI.
read_mcp_resource.use_when: You selected a resource from list_mcp_resources.

text_to_image.purpose: Generate or prepare raster images through the configured local or remote image runtime.
text_to_image.use_when: The user explicitly needs generated bitmap assets. Call media_generation_capabilities first when model or provider-specific parameters matter.

parameters.source: Optional source override such as local, aihubmix, pollinations, or agnes.

text_to_video.purpose: Generate videos through the configured text-to-video runtime.
text_to_video.use_when: The user explicitly needs generated video or motion assets.

parameters.source: Optional source override; inspect media_generation_capabilities for available sources.
parameters.model: Optional video model id/display name from the active provider profile.
parameters.task_id: Provider task id for status retrieval.
parameters.num_frames: Optional frame count; inspect media_generation_capabilities or list_models for provider constraints.

examples: text_to_video(action="generate", prompt="cinematic sunrise over a quiet city")

media_generation_capabilities.purpose: Inspect current image/video generation sources, default models, readiness, model profiles, parameter constraints, and output capabilities.
media_generation_capabilities.use_when: Before selecting non-default media models or provider-specific generation parameters; also useful when media generation fails due to missing config.

task_manager.purpose: Maintain a checklist-first task system for larger work.
task_manager.use_when: The task is multi-step, cross-layer, long-running, or user requested progress tracking.

subagent.purpose: Create and manage isolated SubAgents, background runs, scopes, and selective context.
subagent.use_when: The main Reverie or Computer Controller Agent should delegate bounded coding, research, or verification.

parameters.action: create, delete, set_model, list, delegate, start, status, wait, cancel, remember, context, forget, or clear_context.
parameters.write_scope: Optional bounded write paths; omitted means read-only.
parameters.context_keys: Selected persistent keys to inject into this run.

examples: subagent(action="start", subagent_id="coder", task="write the scoped script", write_scope=["scripts"])

userInput.purpose: Ask the user a blocking question.
userInput.use_when: Credentials, paid resources, destructive operations, deployments, license/business choices, or contradictions block safe progress.
examples: userInput(question="Which deployment target should I use?")

switch_mode.use_when: Another mode materially improves artifacts, continuity, control, or access to a specialized tool surface.
examples: switch_mode(operation="recommend", query="build a playable Godot combat prototype")

context_management.purpose: Compress, truncate, checkpoint, or restore conversation context.

memory_retrieval.purpose: Query structured long-term memory with scope/type/evidence/confidence metadata.
memory_retrieval.use_when: User preferences, project decisions, failures, successful workflows, or prior feedback matter.

parameters.query: Current request or memory search query.
parameters.scope: Optional session, project, workflow, or procedural filter.
parameters.explain: Whether to include ranking reasons and evidence ids.

memory_manager.purpose: List, inspect, correct, delete, or consolidate structured MemoryItems.
memory_manager.use_when: A memory needs explanation, correction, deletion, or manual consolidation from the event store.

parameters.action: list, get, correct, delete, or consolidate.

evolution_feedback.purpose: Record feedback and manage validated LearningProposal records.
evolution_feedback.use_when: User correction or run feedback should improve future workflow memory, retrieval ranking, tool guidance, or prompt digest.

parameters.action: observe, propose, evaluate, apply, or list.

examples: evolution_feedback(action="observe", feedback="Prefer pytest -q for this repo", target="workflow_memory")

game_design_orchestrator.purpose: Compile game prompts into durable request/program/design artifacts and vertical-slice plans.
game_design_orchestrator.use_when: Game design, production planning, scope reduction, or slice generation is needed.

parameters.action: compile_request, compile_program, plan_production, generate_vertical_slice, or related game orchestration action.

game_project_scaffolder.purpose: Create or upgrade runtime/game project foundations and vertical slices.
game_project_scaffolder.use_when: A playable runtime, project scaffold, or system packet is needed.

parameters.action: plan, create, generate_vertical_slice, upgrade_runtime_project, apply_system_packet, or related scaffolding action.

reverie_engine.purpose: Create, inspect, validate, benchmark, and smoke-test Reverie Engine projects.
reverie_engine.use_when: The built-in runtime is the target.

parameters.action: Runtime action such as create_project, validate, benchmark, smoke, video, or renpy.

game_playtest_lab.purpose: Generate playtest plans, quality gates, telemetry schemas, performance budgets, and slice scores.

parameters.action: create_test_plan, run_quality_gates, score_combat_feel, plan_next_iteration, analyze_session_log, or related action.

game_modeling_workbench.purpose: Manage headless model stubs, Blockbench-style data, game_models assisted lanes, registry sync, and runtime imports.
game_modeling_workbench.use_when: Game modeling assets or registry integration are needed.

blender_modeling_workbench.purpose: Control Blender with auditable scripts, plans, exports, renders, and black-box iteration plan evidence.
blender_modeling_workbench.use_when: Blender DCC control, GLB/GLTF export, previews, or production-character scaffold audits are needed.

game_asset_manager.purpose: Track and manage game asset manifests.

game_balance_analyzer.purpose: Analyze or tune game balance data.
game_balance_analyzer.use_when: Stats, economy, progression, or combat balance needs review.

level_design.purpose: Generate or validate level layouts, flow, pathing, placement, and difficulty.
level_design.use_when: Level structure or spatial design is needed.

parameters.action: generate_layout, generate_rooms, check_logic, analyze_difficulty, validate_path, analyze_flow, export_config, npc_placement, or spatial_analysis.

game_config_editor.purpose: Read or write game configuration data.

game_asset_packer.purpose: Package game assets for runtime delivery.
game_asset_packer.use_when: Assets must be gathered, packed, or prepared for runtime.

game_gdd_manager.purpose: Create and maintain game design documents.
game_gdd_manager.use_when: GDD content or game design artifacts are needed.

story_design.purpose: Create and analyze story bibles, quests, NPCs, dialogue trees, factions, pacing, and arcs.
story_design.use_when: Narrative systems or RPG content are needed.

parameters.action: story_bible, questline, npc_profiles, dialogue_tree, faction_matrix, consistency_check, story_pacing, or character_arc.

game_math_simulator.purpose: Run math simulations for game systems.
game_math_simulator.use_when: Probabilities, curves, economies, or progression need numerical checks.

game_stats_analyzer.use_when: Metrics or playtest data need interpretation.

atlas_delivery_orchestrator.purpose: Manage Atlas document contracts, slices, blockers, checkpoints, and closure readiness.
atlas_delivery_orchestrator.use_when: Atlas mode needs durable delivery state.

ask_clarification.purpose: Ask targeted creative or writing brief questions.
ask_clarification.use_when: Writer mode lacks essential style, format, tone, length, POV, tense, genre, or audience information.
examples: ask_clarification(question="What POV and target length should I use?")

novel_context_manager.purpose: Manage long-form writing context and memory.

consistency_checker.use_when: Characters, plot, timeline, or world details need validation.

plot_analyzer.purpose: Analyze plot structure, pacing, character voice, and logical flow.

task_boundary.use_when: Ant mode starts or changes planning, execution, or verification phases.
examples: task_boundary(TaskName="Implement feature", Mode="EXECUTION", TaskSummary="...", TaskStatus="...", PredictedTaskSize=5)

notify_user.purpose: Ask for review, surface blockers, or notify completion in Ant mode.
examples: notify_user(Message="Plan is ready for review", PathsToReview=["artifacts/task.md"], BlockedOnUser=true)

serial_novel.purpose: Create, resume, validate, and complete persistent long-form fiction projects with disk-backed evidence.
serial_novel.use_when: Writer mode receives a novel, serial, continuation, or 100k-character fiction request.

parameters.action: bootstrap, configure, prepare_chapter, commit_chapter, context, status, audit, complete, or list_projects.
parameters.novel_id: Stable project id reused in later prompts.
parameters.chapter: One-based chapter number for chapter actions.
parameters.data: Project bibles, chapter control card, or committed chapter content and state updates.

examples: serial_novel(action="bootstrap", novel_id="glass-tide", title="Glass Tide", brief="...", target_chars=100000)
serial_novel(action="prepare_chapter", novel_id="glass-tide", chapter=1, title="The Rain Market", data={"outline":"..."})

get_app_state.purpose: Capture an app screenshot and indexed accessibility tree.
get_app_state.use_when: Before every Computer Use action in an assistant turn.

parameters.app: Running app name, window title, executable, or PID.
parameters.show_full_text: Whether accessibility text may exceed 500 characters.

list_apps.purpose: List running apps with accessible top-level windows.
list_apps.use_when: Discovering the exact app identifier before observation.

click.purpose: Click an indexed accessibility element or screenshot coordinate.
click.use_when: Activating a desktop control after get_app_state.

parameters.element_index: Preferred target from the accessibility tree.

perform_secondary_action.purpose: Invoke an accessibility action such as Invoke, Toggle, Select, Expand, or Collapse.
perform_secondary_action.use_when: The observed element advertises a suitable secondary action.

scroll.use_when: Moving a scrollable region by pages.

drag.use_when: Operating canvas or slider surfaces without semantic actions.

type_text.purpose: Type literal text into the focused control.
type_text.use_when: An observed editor or field already has focus.

press_key.purpose: Press a key or key combination.
press_key.use_when: Navigation or shortcuts are more reliable than pointing.

parameters.key: xdotool-style key, such as Return or ctrl+s.

set_value.purpose: Set a value through Windows UI Automation.

dynamic_tools.purpose: Tools discovered from configured MCP servers.
dynamic_tools.calling_method: Use the submitted schema exactly before use.

dynamic_tools.purpose: Runtime-plugin tools discovered from protocol-ready plugins.
dynamic_tools.calling_method: Use the submitted schema exactly; common examples include rc_blender_ensure_runtime and rc_blender_run_script.
